public_metrics.notable_visible_detail: One visible review reported waiting six months for an offer.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers value heat-pump advice that explains both the technical solution and the process.
observations.decision_relevance: Heat-pump buyers need plain-language confidence for a complex home upgrade.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers value help with subsidy applications and related paperwork.
observations.decision_relevance: Uncertainty about support and net cost can slow a heat-pump decision.

observations.what_customers_say: Positive heat-pump reviews stress that what was promised matches later operation.
observations.decision_relevance: For skeptical buyers, post-install proof can make the quote feel less speculative.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers praise a process that feels clear and coordinated from advice to installation.
observations.decision_relevance: Process clarity reduces perceived execution risk.

observations.what_customers_say: A visible review described a long wait for an offer, showing that capacity delays can cool interest.
observations.decision_relevance: Silence or unclear timing can weaken intent even when the buyer is interested.

observations.what_customers_say: The review context includes retrofits where buyers need confidence that the solution fits existing buildings.
observations.decision_relevance: Building-specific facts can matter more than generic heat-pump claims.

cross_source_notes: Heat-pump subsidy and operating-cost clarity also appears in public consumer guidance and forum discussions.
Quote-delay sensitivity maps directly to the PoC's stale-quote board concept.
caveats: The long-wait example is a single visible review.
Subsidy information is time-sensitive and must be checked separately before use.
